content.text: This page is to summarize the terms used in SUM SSC

rows: A message that has been selected to require the authentication method
a signal group composed of the Transmit Data Region followed by the Authentication Region
The portion of the payload of an Authenticated Message that contains one or more signals that require authentication
The portion of the payload of an Authenticated Message that contains the authentication content including the MAC
The serial data message containing the Authoritative Counter

A counter contained in the transmitter of an Authenticated Message which is received by an ECU that is a consumer of a Transmit Data Region of an Authenticated Message. This counter is transmitted via serial data communications in the Counter Sync Message
The most recent verified Authoritative Counter received by an ECU that is a consumer of a Transmit Data Region
The most recent received Authoritative Counter that has not yet been verified to be the Active Counter
A calibration table required by SUM_SSC that contains unique data for identification of each Authenticated Message to the security peripheral elements.

content.text: The data buffer used in the MAC generation starts with the 5 Byte long Message_ID, the 64 bit long authoritative counter (i.e. anti-replay counter at the sender side)
